title: VLOOKUP vs INDEX/MATCH: which is better?
meta_desc: VLOOKUP vs INDEX MATCH compared: left lookups, robustness to column inserts, speed, readability, and compatibility across Excel, Google Sheets, and LibreOffice.
intro: INDEX/MATCH was the power user's lookup long before XLOOKUP existed, and it still matters because it runs in every spreadsheet app and every version — while fixing VLOOKUP's worst habits.

table.aspect: Look left of the key column
cols: Yes — MATCH and INDEX ranges are independent
No — the column number goes stale
Two nested calls — harder for beginners
Can be faster — only touches two columns
Universal — works in every version of all three apps

when.use_when: Quick lookups in simple, stable tables where the key is already the first column and nobody inserts columns.
The sheet must work for users on ANY version (including LibreOffice 24.2 and old Excel where XLOOKUP doesn't exist), you need a left-lookup, or the table's columns change over time.

compat_note: All three functions execute correctly in every LibreOffice release we test, all Excel versions, and Google Sheets — INDEX/MATCH is the most portable robust lookup that exists. If everyone's on a modern version, XLOOKUP does everything INDEX/MATCH does with one call.

examples.label: INDEX/MATCH equivalent (works even if B is left of A)
